typical_monthly_usd: $700-$1,200 for Pro Package (single-bay independent AR shop); ACA members report ~$250/mo savings off standard Pro
setup_fee_usd: $399-$899 typical one-time; $399 setup for Auto Care Alliance / MWACA members; ~$500 off standard setup via ACA partnership
min_ad_spend_usd: Not bundled into platform fee; PPC management is included but ad budget is paid separately by client (typical shop spends $500-$2,000/mo on Google Ads on top of Kukui fee) [uncertain]
contract_length_months: 12 (annual term); auto-renews per ToS unless client cancels in writing per Order terms
cancellation_policy: Auto-renews as long as Kukui provides Services. No refunds for any reason. Client remains fully liable for sums due even if they stop using the service mid-term. 30-day data export window after termination, then Kukui has no obligation to maintain client data.
asset_ownership: Kukui-built website is hosted on Kukui infrastructure; multiple Capterra reviewers report being told they could keep the website at signup but were later denied or charged. Domain typically owned by client if client registered it; site CMS, code, and integrations are Kukui's. Migration off platform is non-trivial.
pricing_transparency: Quote-only; pricing page exists but lists no dollar amounts. Public floor of $499/mo comes from Capterra/GetApp/SoftwareFinder listings, not Kukui.com. ACA partnership page is the only public source confirming ~$250/mo + $399 setup discount math.
known_pain_points: Cookie-cutter website templates with shared stock photos across all Kukui clients
Customer service decline: shops cycle through multiple account managers in 12-24 months, each requiring ~2 month ramp
Sales reps allegedly over-promise free features that become paid add-ons at renewal/cancel
Duplicate Google Ads campaigns reported when Kukui signs competing shops in same geo
Same canned social media posts pushed to all clients despite paying for 'custom' content
No refunds policy; client liable for full annual term even if dissatisfied
Sales team turnover (per Glassdoor: 'every salesperson left in a given year')
Disputes over website ownership at cancellation
Dashboard tax-total calc reportedly skews shop financial reporting
notable_clients: Members of Auto Care Alliance (national network)
Members of Midwest Auto Care Alliance (MWACA)
pros: Vertical specialist: built for AR (mechanical) only — terminology, integrations, KPIs are auto-repair native
Google Partner Premier status (2023+) — Google Ads management is legitimately credentialed, unlike Hibu
Deep SMS integrations (Shop-Ware, NAPA TRACS, Tekmetric, Mitchell1) pull RO data into CRM automatically
All-in-one bundle removes vendor sprawl (website, CRM, PPC, reviews, DVI under one login)
Industry-association partnerships (ACA, MWACA, ATI, AutoZone) provide vetted discounts
Postcard direct mail integration is rare and valued by service-reminder workflows
cons: Locked into 12-month auto-renewing contract with no-refund clause
Website is templated; minimal design differentiation between Kukui clients
Account manager turnover undermines strategy continuity
Quote-only pricing — no published tier sheet means shops can't benchmark before sales call
Reported conflict of interest: same vendor running PPC for competing shops in same market
Mostly AR-focused; weak fit for collision repair (CR) workflows (no estimating, DRP, CCC/Mitchell collision integration)
Ad spend not included — true total cost = Kukui fee + Google Ads budget + any add-ons
koffman_relevance: Strong functional upgrade over Hibu for the AR side of Koffman: vertical AR specialization, Google Partner Premier (vs Hibu's iPromote display spam), real SMS/CRM integration with shop management systems, and call tracking that ties leads to ROs. Pricing ($499-$1,200/mo + ad spend + $399-$899 setup) lands roughly even with or below Hibu's blended invoice but with materially better Google Ads execution. However, Kukui does NOT serve the collision side — Koffman would still need a separate CR solution (Turnkey Marketing, BodyShop Booster, or PSG in-house), so Kukui is not a single-vendor Hibu replacement for a combined AR+CR shop. Kukui's 12-month auto-renewing no-refund contract is a similar lock-in risk to Hibu, and website-ownership disputes at cancellation are a documented pattern. Verdict: viable replacement for Hibu on the mechanical side; not a full replacement, and PSG should weigh against Turnkey (auto specialist serving both AR + CR) before recommending.
uncertain: exact tier breakdown above the $499 floor (Starter / Pro / Premier or similar)
min_ad_spend_usd — whether Kukui imposes a minimum Google Ads budget for PPC management to be effective
whether contract is truly 12-month or 24-month for top tier
Koffman-specific quote (no public Kukui quote for Campbellsport WI market)
